sb_prompt: DISCUSS POSSIBLE CHANGES IN THE WAY TRIALS BY JURY ARE CONDUCTED.     FOR EXAMPLE
turns: So, what are your thoughts on potential changes to how trials by jury are conducted?
I think there should be more focus on ensuring jurors are impartial and unbiased before the trial begins.
That's an interesting point. How do you think this can be achieved?
Well, one way could be through extensive background checks and questionnaires to assess their biases or connections to the case.
Yes, that could certainly help ensure an unbiased jury. Do you think there are any other ways we could improve the trial process?
Another idea would be to allow cameras in the courtroom so the public can better understand the legal system and increase trust in it.
Interesting suggestion. What are your thoughts on allowing cameras in the courtrooms?
While it might enhance transparency and public understanding, it could also potentially influence the outcome if sensationalized by the media.
Good point. So, what do you think should be done to balance transparency and fairness in the trial process?
Perhaps limiting camera usage during sensitive parts of the trial, while still providing live broadcast for major milestones, could strike a good balance.
Thank you for sharing your insights. It was great discussing this with you!
You're welcome! It was nice talking with you as well. Have a great day!
Same to you! Take care! Bye! 😊✌️
